title: surprise
description: These words all mean to make somebody feel surprised.

definitions.definition: to give somebody the feeling that you get when something happens that you do not expect or do not understand, or something that you do expect does not happen; to make somebody feel surprised:

examples.en: The outcome didn’t surprise me at all.
examples.cn: 这一结果完全在我的意料之中。

definitions.definition: to surprise somebody suddenly in a way that slightly shocks or frightens them:

examples.en: Sorry, I didn’t mean to startle you.
examples.cn: 抱歉，我不是故意吓你一跳的。

examples.en: Just the huge size of the place amazed her.
examples.cn: 仅仅地方之大就使她十分惊奇。

definitions.definition: (rather informal) (often in newspapers) to surprise or shock somebody so much that they cannot think clearly or speak

examples.cn: 这消息使大家十分惊讶。

patterns: It surprises somebody/startles somebody/amazes somebody/stuns somebody/astonishes somebody/takes somebody aback/astounds somebody
